scope: Script-generation skeleton for Class B. NOT literal templates — a structural formula with per-beat Gemini prompt recipes that reference variables (slot data) and contain tone-calibration examples + variation constraints. One Gemini call per video produces the complete timestamped script.

architecture.model: single Gemini call per generated video
input: filled_slot_data (per-niche channel data + computed money math + content analysis)
architecture.output: JSON with timestamped beat-by-beat script — { intro, niches[], cta }
architecture.downstream: ElevenLabs TTS per beat → WAV files → mix into audio bed per audio-sfx-class-b.json

global_voice_constraints.register: calm, educational, matter-of-fact male documentary narrator

enthusiasm_modifiers.rationale: restrained — not every claim is incredible

vocabulary_taboos: 'Click the link in the description' (alone, as sole CTA)
'I hope to see each other in another one of our videos'
Exposing RPM math in speech (present outcome only — never 'views × RPM = $')

pacing_constraints.rule: each beat narrator text readable in allotted hold_seconds × 1.05 (slight padding for TTS pacing)
pacing_constraints.exception: money_shot card can exceed for emphasis

beats_per_niche.variation_rule: alternate punctuation across niches; never use the same form 3× in a row

beats_per_niche.skip_if: niche label already combined into beat 1 (e.g. 'Number 6, Personality Quizzes')

tone_examples: This channel already has more than 400,000 subscribers.
This channel makes explanation style videos
This channel literally ranks letters and numbers
There is another channel that started making the same kind of videos just 3 months ago
This channel has posted only 20 videos and already gained almost 80,000 subscribers

opener_selection_rule.2nd_or_3rd_channel_of_niche: 'There's another channel that...' OR 'And there's another channel...'

tone_examples: The channel has already gained over 13 million total views.
And their channel has a mind-blowing total of more than 800 million views.
Now, it has gained 50k subscribers and is doing...
beats_per_niche.skip_if: beat 4 already used 'total views' or covered age phrase

tone_examples: Their most popular video has more than 1 million views.
That video got over 1 million views.
this channel made a video called {title} and it got {views} views
beats_per_niche.variation_rule: alternate between 'their most popular video' / 'their top video' / 'this one video' — never same phrase twice in a row across listicle

tone_examples: 29 million views,
10 million views,
8.8 million views...
beats_per_niche.final_phrase_rule: optionally prepend 'and' to the last phrase for natural rhythm

tone_examples: and almost every single upload pulls in hundreds of thousands of views.
And their videos consistently pull really good views.
And their view consistency is amazing.

tone_examples_full_sequences: that one video alone has probably made around
If we assume a $3 RPM, that's
because the videos are long and most viewers... likely are from countries like

card_4: 'that one video alone has probably made around'

geo_context_insertion.insertion: between card 3 and card 4: 'because viewers are from {high_cpm_countries}' (e.g. 'because the videos are long and most viewers likely are from the US and UK')

rpm_modifier_rule.low_rpm_$1_$3: use 'just' or 'Even if we assume' minimizer
rpm_modifier_rule.higher_rpm_$6_$10: use 'if we assume' without minimizer; consider geo-context insertion

tone_examples: This channel simply records gameplay of a funny stickman game and uploads it.
When you look at their videos, the editing style is extremely simple.
They just record the gameplay, add those viral troll or skull face effects...
On top of that, they use viral phonk music in the background...

structure.beat_1: opening recipe summary ('This channel simply...' or 'They take...' or 'When you look at their videos...')
structure.beat_2: editing/production specifics ('They just...' / 'On top of that, they...')
structure.beat_3_to_N: additional elements + outcome ('which makes the audience watch till the end' / 'and keeps the videos engaging')

tone_calibration_by_recipe_type.low_effort_gameplay_screenrec: emphasize 'simply', 'just', 'extremely simple' — drives 'you could do this'
tone_calibration_by_recipe_type.higher_skill_animation_scripted: emphasize 'the storytelling is very strong' — implies effort but achievable

tone_examples: The number one thing you must focus on in this niche is storytelling.
Here's the thing — what makes these channels work is consistency.
What separates the top channels here is timing.

bank: And if you're watching this far, I really appreciate it.
By the way, if you're still here, thank you.
Real quick — if you've made it this far, that means a lot.

beats_per_niche.default_behavior: silent (whoosh SFX + glitch transition does the work)

intro.default_behavior: SKIP preamble — Money Groot's cold-open style — go straight to niche 1's intro_card
preamble_tone_examples: I went through hundreds of faceless YouTube channels...
These {niche_count} faceless niches are quietly making creators...

tone_examples: So, these are the 11 faceless niches.
And each one has huge potential if you're serious about starting a channel.
And if you want to discover 20 more faceless niches,
just click on this video right here.

examples: 'Any one of these could become a real channel'
'just click on this video right here.'
required_structure.rule: must contain 'check out [this/next] video' phrase (17× winner uplift)

explicit_exclusions: 'I hope to see each other in another one of our videos' (0× winners use this)
'click the link in the description' alone (sponsor-read tone)
Money Groot's pre-CTA personal anecdote ('A while back I thought about starting a channel...')
Money Groot's self-promotion fluff ('I'll keep bringing you more valuable content completely free')

within_video.channel_intro_openers: never use 'This channel...' for both channels of the same niche; never the same opener twice in any 3-niche stretch
within_video.money_math_connectors: rotate 'Even if we assume' / 'If we assume' / 'Let's say we assume' across niches
within_video.recipe_demo_openers: rotate 'This channel simply...' / 'When you look at their videos...' / 'What's interesting is...' across niches
within_video.transition_phrases: when used, rotate 'And finally' / 'Moving on' / 'Next up'
within_video.enthusiasm_modifiers: 'already' / 'more than' / 'literally' — can be reused but never 3× in same paragraph

across_generations.tracking_window: rolling window of last 50 narrator phrases per beat_id
across_generations.retention_policy: purge entries older than 7 days OR keep most recent 50 per beat_id (whichever smaller)
across_generations.usage: passed to Gemini as variation_history_for_beat_{beat_id} — Gemini avoids exact reuse, paraphrases instead
across_generations.scope_keyed_by: beat_id (channel_intro phrases tracked separately from money_math connectors)

user_prompt_includes: filled_slot_data (per-niche channel data + computed money math + content analysis labels + concept tags)
variation_history (rolling-window of recent phrases per beat_id)

next_checkpoints: pipeline-architecture.md — worker topology orchestrating the whole stack
icon-library-asset-spec.md — AI-image-gen prompts for the 15 line-drawing icons
script-skeleton-class-a.md — brand-studio template when we add Class A
